title: Trade Queens to Save the Day
setup: Black's queen on **d3** is attacked by both the white queen on e2 and the bishop on c4. She must trade now or be lost.
hint: When your queen is attacked twice and defended zero times, swap her off — don't dance.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is a **forced trade** — a survival tactic, not a winning combination. Black's queen is attacked by **two** white pieces (queen e2 and bishop c4) and defended by **none**. If Black does nothing, White plays {{Qxd3}} (or {{Bxd3}}) winning the queen for nothing.

The only good move is to **initiate the trade on Black's terms**: capture the white queen.

Black plays {{Qxe2}}.

## White's Forced Reply

White must recapture or lose the queen for nothing:

- {{Bxe2}} — the bishop on c4 slides to e2 and recaptures. This is the only legal recapture (no rook or knight reaches e2 in one move).

Material after the dust settles: equal. Black has converted a losing position into a balanced middlegame.

## Pattern to Memorize

> When your queen is doubly attacked and undefended, the question changes from *"Can I win material?"* to *"How do I lose the least?"*

A queen trade with capture neutralizes the attack and equalizes material. Always check this option before retreating — sometimes the simplest swap is the best resource.
